item.title: Version 1.27.5
item.description:
Added

  • Lists that write themselves. Press Return on a bullet, numbered, or checklist item and the next one starts automatically (Return on an empty item ends the list). Tab and Shift-Tab indent and outdent list items.
  • ⌘B, ⌘I, and ⌘K in notes. Bold or italicize the selection (press again to undo it), or wrap it as a link with the cursor ready for the URL.
  • Richer note rendering. Numbered lists, blockquotes, and horizontal rules (---) now render properly instead of showing raw markdown.

Fixed

  • Typing in long notes stays fast. Notes now save shortly after you pause typing instead of re-encrypting the whole store on every keystroke — and still save instantly when you switch notes, close, or quit.
  • Notes look better. Headings render at their proper sizes, bullets get real bullet points, and line spacing gives text room to breathe. Notes pasted with Windows-style line endings no longer collapse into one giant heading.

item.title: Version 1.27.6
item.description:
Added

  • Notes read and edit on one surface. No more flip between a reading view and an editor: a note opens looking fully formatted, clicking in places the cursor right there, and only the line you're on reveals its markdown markers — bold, headings, and links stay styled as you type, Obsidian-style. Esc puts the note back to reading.
  • Click a checkbox to toggle it. Click the box of a - [ ] item to check or uncheck it — no need to edit the text. Checked items show struck through.
  • Find inside a note. ⌘F opens a find bar scoped to the note you're reading (⌘F elsewhere still searches your lists). Esc dismisses the bar, then the note.
  • Move and delete lines. ⌥↑ and ⌥↓ move the current line (or selection) up and down; ⌘⇧K deletes it.
  • [[ closes itself. Typing [[ adds the closing ]] with the cursor between, the link suggestions still pop up, and picking one never leaves stray brackets.
  • A details sidebar for notes. A toggle in the note's top-right corner opens a column with where the note is linked from, created/updated times, its folder and source, and word counts — collapse it and the note takes back the full width.
  • Working undo in notes. ⌘Z / ⇧⌘Z now work in the note editor, scoped to the note you're editing.

Changed

  • Bullets look like bullets. List dashes render as • at every nesting level, wrapped lines align under their item's text, and blockquotes sit indented with dimmed text.

Fixed

  • Sync can't interrupt your typing. A sync arriving while you're editing a note no longer replaces the text under your cursor (which could jump the caret and clear undo) — it waits until you leave the note, and your newer edits always win.
  • Typing in big notes is smoother. Each keystroke now restyles only the line you changed instead of the whole note, which also removes a subtle flicker when moving between lines.

item.title: Version 1.28.0
item.description:
Added

  • One shared Notes editor across Mac and Android. Notes now use the same Markdown engine and document format on both platforms, with headings, bold text, lists, task items, and [[wikilinks]] behaving consistently.
  • Notes are agent-accessible. Authorized agents can create, read, and append to notes through the encrypted sync service without needing an interactive app sign-in.

Changed

  • Notes stay canonical Markdown. Editing, syncing, and agent operations preserve the plain-text source so notes remain portable and future clients can use the same data without translating a proprietary editor format.

Fixed

  • The Android editor fills the available screen and selects text correctly. The shared editor no longer collapses to a zero-height WebView, and touch selection—including double-tap word selection—works on phone.

item.title: Version 1.29.0
item.description:
Added

  • Choose your dictation engine. Settings ▸ Dictation has a new Local engine picker naming the exact model that transcribes on your Mac: Apple SpeechAnalyzer (the macOS 26 system model, the only one that honors My words), NVIDIA Parakeet v3 (a one-time ~600 MB download and the fastest option, 25 European languages), or OpenAI Whisper large-v3-turbo (~630 MB, about 100 languages). The downloaded engines run fully offline — audio never touches disk or leaves your Mac.
  • Dictation on macOS 14 and 15. Pick Parakeet or Whisper and voice dictation no longer requires macOS 26 (Apple silicon Macs).

Changed

  • Hosted dictation returns faster. Audio now uploads compressed — about 8× smaller — so transcripts come back sooner, especially for longer dictations and on slower connections.

Fixed

  • The first dictation after launch no longer fails with "No audio from…". A just-woken microphone could deliver pure silence for the whole first session (and the second try would mysteriously work). Slash now detects the dead start and restarts capture mid-recording without losing any words.
  • A brief network blip no longer downgrades a dictation. Hosted transcription retries once before falling back to on-device.

item.title: Version 1.30.0
item.description:
Added

  • The standard editing shortcuts, in Notes. ⌘B and ⌘I toggle bold and italic (and untangle themselves correctly on already-styled text), ⌘K makes a link, ⇧⌘L toggles a checkbox, ⌘1–⌘3 set heading levels, and pasting a URL over selected text turns it into a link.
  • Find in note. ⌘F opens find and replace inside the note editor.
  • Adjustable note text size. ⌘+ and ⌘− zoom the editor text, ⌘0 resets, and your choice sticks across launches.
  • Spelling, like every Mac text field. The note editor now underlines misspellings in red and applies system autocorrect as you type.

Changed

  • Notes read clean. Markdown markers get out of the way while you read: bold shows as bold without the asterisks, heading hashes hide, dashes render as round bullets, finished tasks cross themselves out, and wrapped list lines align under their text. Put the caret on a line to reveal and edit its markers — the note itself stays plain Markdown.
  • Links open with a plain click. [[wikilinks]] and web links in a note open directly — no modifier key needed.
  • A quieter Library. The notes list drops its subtitle line and the note's info column drops its header, leaving the content itself.

Fixed

  • Typing in notes is fast again. Every keystroke was doing whole-document work behind the scenes, which made long notes feel sluggish — several layers of that are gone, and deleting through indented bullets no longer makes the cursor flutter.
  • Bold always renders. Selecting a word with a stray space or a list dash and pressing ⌘B used to produce styling that could never display; the selection now snaps to what can actually be bolded.
  • Note text was nearly invisible in mixed dark/light setups. With the system in dark mode and Slash in light, the editor painted light text on a light background.
  • A crashed note pane recovers itself. If the editor's underlying web process died, the pane went blank and silently swallowed typing until you switched notes; it now reloads in place.

item.title: Version 1.30.1
item.description:
Fixed

  • Deleting through bullets no longer makes the cursor jiggle. 1.30.0's spellcheck also switched on macOS's inline autocorrect, whose suggestion bubbles and correction-replays fought the cursor during fast deletes and made creating a new bullet feel slow. Autocorrect and auto-capitalization are now off in the note editor; the red spelling underlines stay.

item.title: Version 1.31.0
item.description:
Added

  • Images in notes. Paste, drag in, or insert an image and it lives right inside the note, rendered where you put it and stored encrypted like everything else.

Fixed

  • Enter on a bullet no longer skips a line. Pressing Return at the end of a list item now starts the next item right away, instead of dropping a blank row that the cursor jumped past.
  • Deleting a bullet back to the start no longer jiggles the cursor. Backspacing through the “- ” now removes the marker in one press (or outdents a nested item) instead of leaving invisible spaces the cursor floated over.
  • Indenting a list item is instant again. Pressing Enter and then Tab in quick succession no longer lags, and Tab now indents a full level so nesting is easy to see.
  • Typing in a long note stays smooth. The editor no longer rebuilds the whole window on every keystroke, so fast typing and editing keep up with you.

item.title: Version 1.32.0
item.description:
Added

  • Wiki-link suggestions are always current. Typing [[ now looks up your notes live, so a note you created seconds ago shows up in the autocomplete immediately.

Changed

  • Typing in notes is dramatically faster. The editor was rebuilt so keystrokes go straight to the text — no more app-wide work on every character. Typing, Tab, and switching notes all land in a few milliseconds.
  • Tab behaves like a proper outliner. Tab nests a bullet one level under the item above it (and only then), Shift-Tab un-nests it — and tabbing can never accidentally turn your list into a code block again.

Fixed

  • No more flash of raw markdown while typing. Bullets, headings, and checked-off tasks style themselves the instant you type them, instead of showing - or # for a beat first.
  • Typing a dash under a paragraph no longer blows it up into a heading. Starting a bullet below text used to momentarily restyle the paragraph above as a giant title; headings now come only from #.
  • Quick captures can't be lost to fast clicks. Clicking away from the capture window a moment after typing now saves exactly what you typed, and quitting the app mid-sentence keeps every character.
  • Notes edits, syncs, and agent updates can no longer overwrite each other. A round of hardening closed several rare timing windows where a note update from sync or a capture routed into an open note could clash with what you were typing.

item.title: Version 1.32.1
item.description:
Changed

  • Notes open instantly. The editor now warms up at launch and is reused as you move between notes, so opening a note no longer has a brief loading pause.
  • Tab always indents. Press Tab anywhere on a list item to nest it another level — it no longer has to sit directly under another item, and deeply-indented lines stay proper list items.
  • Indenting is perfectly smooth. The word, the bullet, and the cursor now move together on the same frame when you press Tab, instead of one trailing a beat behind.

Fixed

  • Notes can't be lost to Undo after switching. Pressing ⌘Z right after switching to a different note could overwrite it with the previous note's text — fixed.
  • Typing in the Library is never interrupted. A background refresh could pull focus off the note mid-sentence; the editor keeps it now.
  • Fixed a formatting crash triggered by a link with an empty or just-deleted label.
  • Copying a snippet is safe again — a trailing newline can no longer auto-run when you paste it into a terminal.

item.title: Version 1.32.2
item.description:
Added

  • Start a bullet with just a dash. Type - on an empty line and Slash adds the space for you, so you're straight into the item. One Backspace gives you back a lone dash (for a --- divider).
  • Shift-Tab out of a list. Shift-Tab at the very start of a bullet removes the bullet and drops the line back to plain text.
  • Blockquotes render. A line starting with > now shows a clean left bar instead of the raw marker.
  • Brackets and quotes auto-close. Type (, [, or " and the closer is added for you — and this plays nicely with [[ wiki-link autocomplete.
  • Wiki-links read cleanly. [[Note]] now hides its brackets in the text and opens the note when you click it; the raw form comes back on the line you're editing.
  • Sentence case as you type. The first letter of a sentence capitalizes itself. One Backspace undoes it, so a deliberately-lowercase line (or iOS) costs a single keystroke.
  • The cursor blinks again.

Fixed

  • The cursor and bullet stay locked together. On macOS the caret could paint a step ahead of its bullet while the bullet lagged behind; the editor now keeps the caret, bullet, and text on the same frame so they move as one.

item.title: Version 1.32.3
item.description:
Fixed

  • Click a wiki-link to open it. A plain click on a [[linked note]] in your text now jumps straight to that note.
  • Spellcheck underlines are back. Misspelled words get the red squiggle again as you type.
  • Opening the first note is instant. The editor now finishes warming up at launch, so the first note you open no longer has a brief hitch.
  • Arrow keys move through your notes. Up/down now walk the notes list (and the clipboard and dictation lists) after you click into it, matching the main Library list.

item.title: Version 1.32.4
item.description:
Fixed

  • Clicking a wiki-link really opens it now. The previous fix didn't take on a real click; a [[linked note]] in your text now jumps to that note the moment you click it.
  • Arrow keys really move through your lists now. After you click a note (or a clipboard or dictation item), ↑/↓ walk the selection through the list — matching the main Library list — instead of doing nothing or moving the sidebar.

item.title: Version 1.33.0
item.description:
Added

  • A / menu inside your notes. Type / in a note (on the Mac) for a menu of blocks (headings, lists, task lists, callouts, code, quotes, dividers, dates), inline formatting (bold, italic, strikethrough, highlight, inline code, links), and your whole command library — inserted right where you're typing.
  • Link a note — or create it — as you type [[. The note picker is redesigned, and when the title doesn't exist yet a Create "…" row makes the note and links it in one keystroke.
  • Real, clickable checkboxes. Task lines (- [ ]) render as actual checkboxes — click one to check or uncheck it.
  • A formatting toolbar for selected text. Highlight text in a note and a small bar of bold / italic / link / highlight / code appears above it.

Changed

  • Notes is the home screen. The Library opens on your Notes by default, and Notes sits at the top of the sidebar.
  • Collapsible sidebar sections. Library, History, and Folders each get a disclosure arrow to collapse or expand; your choice sticks.
  • Roomier notes. More breathing room after bullets, cleanly aligned checkboxes, and a lighter empty-note prompt — "Untitled note" and "Write something or press '/' for options".

Fixed

  • Opening a note no longer hitches. The first note you opened used to freeze for a second or two while the editor warmed up; it now boots ahead of time.
  • Blank notes don't pile up. Create a note and leave it without typing anything and it's quietly discarded instead of becoming a stray "Untitled" row — and a note you actually started is never lost.
  • Pointer cursor on backlinks and a note's source link, so it's clear they're clickable.
